name: Y1-P01 — Athletic Foundation + Throwing Standard
description:
Stage: Year 1 / 10U Foundation
Use simple language, short reps, and clear visible targets. Correct one thing at a time.

Objectives:
- Build athletic foundation + throwing standard inside a 2-hour high-rep practice.
- Create game-like decisions instead of line-standing drills.
- Train players to communicate before the ball is hit.
- Finish with measurable competition and player homework.

Stations:
- Station 1: Hitting: middle line drives — 9 minutes + 1 minute transition. End with a pressure rep.
- Station 2: Fielding: triangle footwork — 9 minutes + 1 minute transition. End with a pressure rep.
- Station 3: Bunting: dead ball zones — 9 minutes + 1 minute transition. End with a pressure rep.
- Station 4: Battery: receive/block/command — 9 minutes + 1 minute transition. End with a pressure rep.

Coach details:
- Keep station groups small; no more than 3-4 players idle at any time.
- Use visible cones/targets and score every station so focus stays high.
- Stop only for safety or a team-wide correction; otherwise coach inside the rep.
- End each station with a challenge rep under pressure.
- Metrics to Track
- Clean throw percentage
- Routine ground-ball conversion
- Correct sign recognition
- Baserunning decision score
- Communication score
- Battery command/block score
- Player Language
- Know your job before the pitch.
- Move early. Talk loud. Recover fast.
- Win the next pitch, not the last mistake.

blocks.detail: Sprint mechanics, hip mobility, shuffle/crossover, reaction start.
Wrist, power position, shuffle, quick exchange, tag/throw. Track clean throws.
Walk-through and half-speed reps for athletic foundation + throwing standard. Require every player to state her job.
9 minutes each + fast transition. Stations: Hitting: middle line drives; Fielding: triangle footwork; Bunting: dead ball zones; Battery: receive/block/command.
Live reads from each base, decision cues, slides, returns, and scoring reps.
Coach gives signs and defensive calls. Score execution, communication, and decision quality.
One team standard, one individual homework assignment, one confidence message.
